fact_nuggets.text: Poison Point (Japanese: Poison Thorns) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Poisons attackers. MD T D S Poisons an attacker who lands a direct-contact attack. B S L Conq. Contact with the Pokemon may poison the attacker. MD GtI May inflict the Poisoned status on the attacker who hits it with a direct attack. * When the Pokemon is hit with a direct attack like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker is sometimes afflicted with the Poisoned status condition! * S MD When the Pokemon is hit with a contact move like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker sometimes gets poisoned! MD RT DX When the Pokemon is hit with a contact move, like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker sometimes gets poisoned.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation III 1.1.2 Generation IV onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a move that makes contact , there is a 1/3 chance that the attacking Pokemon will become poisoned . This Ability cannot poison Poison-type or Steel-type Pokemon. If a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact (such as Fury Swipes ), each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Poison Point to poison is lowered to 30%. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Poison Point has no effect outside of battle.

This Ability cannot poison Poison-type or Steel-type Pokemon. If a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact (such as Fury Swipes ), each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Poison Point to poison is lowered to 30%.
Has a 30% chance of poisoning attacking Pokemon on contact.
